sentence: Here is the SOAP note based on the transcript:
Subjective:

HPI: The patient, Wayne, presents with a non-healing wound on his right foot, approximately 1-2 inches in diameter, located on the dorsal aspect of the lateral right foot, proximal to the right fifth MTP joint.
He reports the wound has been present for 2-3 weeks and has not improved despite previous treatment with antibiotics.
The patient denies any trauma to the foot and reports that the wound is painless.
Past Medical History: The patient has a history of diabetes, with an HbA1c of 8%.
He has not had any recent issues with his diabetes management.
He also mentions that he used to see a podiatrist, but they moved and he has not been able to find another one.
Review of Systems: The patient denies any other symptoms such as fever, chills, drainage, or odor from the wound.
Current Medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital Signs: Temperature 98.1°F, heart rate 72, respirations 16, blood pressure 110/65.
Physical Exam: There is a 1x2 inch circular wound on the dorsal aspect of the lateral right foot, with yellow slough present and minimal granulation tissue.
There is no surrounding erythema or cellulitis, no fluid collection, and no necrosis.
The wound does not have an odor.
Bony exposure is not appreciated.
Vascular exam reveals palpable bilateral femoral and popliteal pulses, but no palpable DP or PT pulses.
Doppler signs are present.
Test Results: Right foot X-ray shows no evidence of osteomyelitis.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a diabetic foot ulcer on the right foot, with evidence of non-healing despite previous treatment with antibiotics.
Problem list: Diabetic foot ulcer; Suspected poor blood supply to the foot; Diabetes mellitus; [NOT MENTIONED]
Plan:
I prescribed Collagenase ointment to be applied to the wound once daily and covered with a dry sterile dressing.
I will perform a debridement in the office to remove dead tissue.
I will also order an Ankle Brachial Index (ABI) to assess blood supply to the foot.
The patient will wear a surgical shoe to take pressure off the area.
I do not think any further antibiotics are needed at this time.
I will see the patient in 2 weeks to reassess the wound.
I may refer the patient to a vascular specialist if the ABI indicates poor blood supply.
Follow-up: I will see the patient in 2 weeks to reassess the wound.
